title: Rural Innovation and Small Business Support Act
short_title: Rural Innovation and Small Business Support Act
plain_summary: This bill helps small businesses in rural areas get more chances to receive government money for research projects. It tells government agencies that they must do a better job of letting rural businesses know about funding programs that are available to them. The Small Business Administration has to change their rules within ninety days to make sure rural small businesses can easily find out about and join these programs. The goal is to make sure rural areas get a fair chance at government research funding.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Small Business, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for considerati

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Rural Innovation and Small Business Support Act.

sections.title: Section 2. SBIR and STTR Program Rural Community Outreach
sections.text: This section changes the Small Business Act to require better outreach to rural areas. The Small Business Administration (the government office that helps small businesses) must update their rules within ninety days after this law passes. The new rules will require all government agencies that run SBIR programs (Small Business Innovation Research programs that give money to small companies for research) to do more work to reach out to rural communities. These agencies must make sure small businesses in rural areas know about these programs and can participate in them. The same requirement applies to STTR programs (Small Business Technology Transfer programs that help small companies work with research institutions). Government agencies running these programs must also do more outreach work in rural communities to help small businesses there participate.
